item.title: Diablo 4 Lord of Hatred Release Times, Editions & Price, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Blizzard is getting ready to unleash Diablo 4 Lord of Hatred from deep within, and if you’re looking forward to the expansion, Insider Gaming has all the key release times and an explainer of the DLC’s price.

Diablo 4 Lord of Hatred is the 2026 follow-up to 2025’s Vessel of Hatred. Mephisto takes center stage, but there’s also a lot of hype for the Paladin class and the Warlock, and it’s just a good time to be a Diablo fan!

All Diablo 4 Lord of Hatred Release Times Worldwide

Blizzard has confirmed that Diablo 4 Lord of Hatred goes live at 4 PM PDT on April 27—and we have other dates and times around the world.

  • More Insider Gaming: How To Get Argent Veil In Diablo 4—Best Farm Method, Explained

In our Diablo 4 Season 12 and Diablo Season 13 hub, we disclosed a time based on previous information. Well, Blizzard has put an end to any speculation by outing the full dates and times.

Check our table below to see when you get to play Lord of Hatred.

How Much Does Diablo 4 Lord of Hatred Cost? All Editions & Prices, Explained

The Diablo 4 Lord of Hatred expansion costs $39.99 or £35.99 for the Standard Edition.

If you’re just after the basic version of Lord of Hatred, you’ll only need $39.99 or £35.99. However, other editions will set you back even more!

  • More Insider Gaming: Diablo 4 Lord of Hatred’s Opening Cinematic Can Be Watched Right Now

Check out these full prices and editions right here.

Get ready for a huge bump in Diablo 4’s popularity with every Sanctuary-seeker returning for Diablo 4 Lord of Hatred. Let us know which aspect of the expansion DLC has you the most pumped via our Insider Gaming Discord.

Also, check out the Diablo 4 Lord of Hatred DLC being rated for Nintendo Switch in Indonesia.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: Will Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Be On Xbox Game Pass?
item.content:encoded:
Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced is finally on its way, and plenty of you out there must be wondering if it will arrive on Xbox Game Pass—after all, who wouldn’t love to pick up the remake for free?

Xbox Game Pass has been the centre of attention lately, with talks of new tiers and reduced prices for Ultimate and PC subscribers. With Xbox clearly wanting to make Game Pass the place to play, adding Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced to the service would certainly be a huge step in that direction.

But is that on the cards, or will you be forced to buy it at full price? The answers are down below.

Will You Be Able To Play Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced On Xbox Game Pass?

Unfortunately, Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced is not being advertised as an Xbox Game Pass inclusion, with everything pointing towards the title not being a day-one arrival in the catalogue.

It’s a crying shame, as it’d be an excellent addition to the hundreds of games already available on the service. But it’s clear that Ubisoft think Black Flag Resynced can stand on its own two feet, and based on the hype surrounding the game, they’re probably right.

  • More Insider Gaming: Will Assassin’s Creed: Black Flag Resynced Have DLC Or Multiplayer?

With all the combat changes and collector’s edition, it’s likely Ubisoft want to encourage people to buy the game day one, instead of relying on a subscription service like Xbox Game Pass. However, that doesn’t mean the game’s future is set in stone…

Will Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Ever Come To Xbox Game Pass?

Never say never, as Assassin’s Creed titles have a long history of arriving on Xbox Game Pass, though it’s just not alongside their launch dates.

At the time of writing, tons of Assassin’s Creed titles are available on Xbox Game Pass Ultimate, thanks to its partnered subscription to Ubisoft+. That grants you access to a whole variety of Assassin’s Creed games, ranging from some of the more recent titles, like Valhalla and Mirage, to some of the classics, like Unity or Brotherhood.

  • More Insider Gaming: Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Platforms, Explained

In fact, you can even play the original Assassin’s Creed Black Flag on there right now! So, if you can’t wait for the remake and want to dive into the game, you can always cash in an Ultimate Game Pass subscription and set sail right away.

But, if you want to experience the remake for all of its updates and additions, it looks like you’ll have to buy the game day one, or wait a good while for it to potentially arrive on Game Pass in the distant future.

Will you be playing Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced when it releases? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Assassin’s Creed coverage, check out some of the following:

  • Don’t Worry, Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Isn’t An RPG
  • Edward Kenway’s Voice Actor Returns For Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion
